description: A horizontal composite/event image. On the left, a man in a dark suit with a maroon tie stands at a wooden podium bearing the round seal of the U.S. Department of Labor ('DEPARTMENT OF LABOR / UNITED STATES OF AMERICA' with an eagle-and-shield emblem), with two American flags behind him and a patriotic blue backdrop. The backdrop displays a designed graphic with three white stars and red/white/blue styling reading 'INVESTING IN TOMORROW'S WORKFORCE - Through Registered Apprenticeship'. On the right side stands a printed event poster (foamcore sign) framed by waving American-flag graphics, reading 'BUILDING THE AI-READY WORKFORCE THROUGH REGISTERED APPRENTICESHIP - April 29, 2026', a banner 'National APPRENTICESHIP WEEK - APRIL 26 - MAY 2, 2026', and a blue directional arrow 'THIS WAY TO EVENT', with a small DOL seal. The style is patriotic, polished governmental event branding (red/white/blue). This is a DOL workforce/apprenticeship promotional image, not an immigration meme; it projects the administration's pro-worker, AI-ready economic messaging to a general/policy audience. Designed agency graphic (combining a candid podium photo with heavy graphic design).
notable_text: Investing in Tomorrow's Workforce Through Registered Apprenticeship. Building the AI-Ready Workforce Through Registered Apprenticeship, April 29, 2026. National Apprenticeship Week, April 26 - May 2, 2026. This Way to Event.
